name: Budapest Karate Akadémia
description: One of Budapest's premier karate academies, offering training for all ages and skill levels in traditional Shotokan and sport karate.
firstTrainingInfo: No prior experience required. Just show up in comfortable sportswear — we'll take care of the rest.
equipmentNeeded: Comfortable sportswear. Karate gi available to borrow for the first class.

name: Fight Club Budapest
description: A top-tier MMA gym with professional coaches in BJJ, boxing, and mixed martial arts. Home to several Hungarian national champions.
firstTrainingInfo: First session is a technique-only introduction — no sparring. Coach evaluates before any contact training.
equipmentNeeded: Mouthguard and groin guard required from session 2. Gloves available to borrow on your first visit.

name: Muay Thai Budapest
description: The leading Muay Thai gym in Hungary, with certified instructors trained in Thailand. Classes for beginners through advanced fighters.
firstTrainingInfo: First class is fitness + basic technique only, zero contact. You'll leave sweaty and smiling.
equipmentNeeded: Sports clothes and boxing wraps. Gloves available to borrow for the first few sessions.

name: BJJ Factory Budapest
description: Dedicated Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu academy affiliated with a Gracie-certified school. Open mat sessions every weekend.
firstTrainingInfo: Absolute beginners welcome. We run a dedicated Fundamentals class every Monday — perfect starting point.
equipmentNeeded: Comfortable clothes for the first class. BJJ gi loaner available. Rash guard recommended from week 2.

name: Budokan Martial Arts Center
description: A large martial arts complex combining traditional Karate with modern MMA training. Features a dedicated ring and mat area.
firstTrainingInfo: Trial class is completely free. Children's classes run parallel to adult sessions so families can train together.
equipmentNeeded: Comfortable sports clothes. All equipment provided during the trial period.

name: Iron Fist Boxing Gym
description: Classic boxing gym in central Budapest with a rich history. Offers amateur and professional boxing training programs.
firstTrainingInfo: First class is pad work and bag work only — no sparring. Coach approval required before any contact.
equipmentNeeded: Hand wraps and boxing gloves (available to purchase or rent on site).

name: Tiger Muay Thai & BJJ Budapest
description: An affiliate of the world-famous Tiger Muay Thai camp in Phuket. Offers Muay Thai and Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu under expert guidance.
firstTrainingInfo: First two sessions are technique-only. Sparring begins only after coach assessment — your safety is our priority.
equipmentNeeded: Shorts and rash guard for BJJ. Boxing wraps and mouthguard for Muay Thai.

name: Warriors MMA Club
description: A well-rounded MMA facility offering boxing, Muay Thai, and mixed martial arts coaching. Hosts regular sparring events.
firstTrainingInfo: We welcome complete beginners. Separate beginner classes run before main sessions — no experience needed.
equipmentNeeded: Sports clothes for the first class. Full gear list provided after your first session.

name: Kyokushin Budapest
description: Specializing in Kyokushin full-contact karate, this dojo develops discipline, strength, and fighting spirit.
firstTrainingInfo: Kyokushin is full-contact, but your first class is conditioning + basics only — absolutely zero contact.
equipmentNeeded: Karate gi or comfortable sports clothes for the first session.

name: Octagon Fight Center
description: A dedicated combat sports facility with a full-size octagon cage. Professional-level training for serious competitors.
firstTrainingInfo: Serious training environment. Free trial class available — come and see if it fits your goals.
equipmentNeeded: Shorts and rash guard for BJJ. MMA gloves and shin guards for MMA classes.

name: Sparta Boxing Academy
description: Youth-focused boxing academy producing regional and national champions. Programs available for ages 8 and up.
firstTrainingInfo: Youth-first gym. All ages welcome. First class is completely free — no commitment.
equipmentNeeded: Comfortable clothes. Hand wraps and gloves available to borrow.

name: Cobra BJJ & Submission Wrestling
description: Focuses exclusively on BJJ and submission grappling. Small class sizes ensure personalized coaching.
firstTrainingInfo: Small class sizes mean personal attention from day one. Bring a good attitude — we'll handle the rest.
equipmentNeeded: Rash guard or t-shirt and shorts. Gi available to borrow for the first session.

name: Ferencváros Küzdősport Klub
description: A community-based sports club in the heart of Ferencváros, offering boxing, karate, and MMA for locals of all backgrounds.
firstTrainingInfo: Community club open to everyone. Relaxed atmosphere, zero judgment. Just show up.
equipmentNeeded: Any comfortable sportswear. All equipment available to borrow.

name: Buda Combat Sports
description: Buda's go-to gym for Muay Thai and MMA. Emphasizes fitness and self-defense alongside competitive training.
firstTrainingInfo: Strong self-defense focus. Dedicated women's class every Wednesday evening — a safe and empowering space.
equipmentNeeded: Sports clothes. Boxing wraps and gloves available to borrow.

name: Grappling House
description: Premier grappling facility offering BJJ and wrestling-based MMA. Home of the Budapest Grappling Open tournament.
firstTrainingInfo: Home of the Budapest Grappling Open. Serious competitors welcome — but all levels are trained here.
equipmentNeeded: Rash guard and shorts for no-gi; gi for BJJ classes. Both available to borrow for a first visit.

name: Angyalföld Boxing Club
description: A storied boxing club in District XIII with over 40 years of history producing Olympic-level talent.
firstTrainingInfo: A legendary club with high standards. Dedicated beginner program gets you ready at your own pace.
equipmentNeeded: Hand wraps and boxing gloves. Available at the gym store.

name: Zuglói Karate Dojo
description: Traditional Shotokan karate dojo in Zugló. Great for families and children, with a structured grading system.
firstTrainingInfo: Perfect for families. Children's and adult classes run simultaneously — parents and kids can train together.
equipmentNeeded: Comfortable clothes. White karate gi recommended after the first month.

name: Kelenföld Fight Academy
description: A modern facility in south-west Buda offering Muay Thai, BJJ, and boxing classes throughout the day.
firstTrainingInfo: Morning and evening classes available — perfect if you have a busy schedule. Full equipment rental on site.
equipmentNeeded: Sports clothes for the first class. The gym has full equipment rental for all disciplines.

name: Óbuda Martial Arts Club
description: A friendly dojo in Óbuda teaching Karate and BJJ to children and adults. Strong focus on respect and discipline.
firstTrainingInfo: Strong focus on respect and discipline. Great first martial arts experience for both kids and adults.
equipmentNeeded: Comfortable clothes. Karate gi required after the first grading exam.

name: Pesterzsébet Fight Gym
description: Affordable MMA and boxing gym in District XX. Open evenings and weekends for working professionals.
firstTrainingInfo: Affordable and no-fuss. Evening classes designed around working schedules. No prior experience needed.
equipmentNeeded: Comfortable sports clothes. Basic equipment available to borrow.

name: Champion Muay Thai Gym
description: Established by a former Thai boxing champion, this gym offers authentic Muay Thai conditioning and technique classes.
firstTrainingInfo: Founded by a champion — expect high-level technical coaching from day one. Conditioning-heavy first sessions.
equipmentNeeded: Boxing wraps and shorts. Gloves and shin guards available to rent.

name: Újpest Küzdősport
description: Versatile martial arts school in District IV, known for its passionate community and dedicated coaching staff.
firstTrainingInfo: Passionate coaching staff and welcoming atmosphere. Free trial class for all new members.
equipmentNeeded: Comfortable sportswear for the trial. Equipment list provided after sign-up.

name: Ground Zero BJJ Academy
description: A pure BJJ academy with black belt instructors and competitive teams at the national and international level.
firstTrainingInfo: Black belt instructors with international competition experience. Beginners placed in dedicated fundamentals program.
equipmentNeeded: BJJ gi or rash guard + shorts for no-gi classes. Available to borrow for a first session.

name: Kispest Combat Club
description: A full-service combat sports club in District XIX offering boxing, Muay Thai, and MMA to competitors and fitness enthusiasts.
firstTrainingInfo: Full introductory program for every discipline. Come to the first class and we'll set up the right path for you.
equipmentNeeded: Sports clothes for the first class. Equipment list provided after your first session.

name: Pasarét Karate & Fitness
description: A welcoming karate school in the hills of Buda, combining traditional martial arts with modern fitness methods.
firstTrainingInfo: Very welcoming atmosphere. Combined karate and fitness — great for those wanting both discipline and a workout.
equipmentNeeded: Gym clothes. Karate gi is optional and can be purchased later.

name: Józsefváros MMA Factory
description: A no-nonsense MMA gym in District VIII with a reputation for producing serious fighters. Daily open mat included with membership.
firstTrainingInfo: Serious and committed training environment. Daily open mat included. Come ready to work.
equipmentNeeded: Full MMA gear required from week 2. Available to purchase on site or borrow for the first session.

name: Budapest Muay Thai Center
description: Specializing in Muay Thai and MMA crossover training. Weekly seminars by visiting instructors from Thailand and the US.
firstTrainingInfo: Weekly guest seminars from international coaches. Beginners are integrated smoothly into regular classes.
equipmentNeeded: Shorts and t-shirt. Gloves and Thai pads available to borrow.
